attraction_name: Nanjing City Wall Taicheng Scenic Area
description: The best-preserved section of the Ming Dynasty city wall, offering a view of the beautiful scenery of Xuanwu Lake from the top.

attraction_name: Purple Mountain Observatory, Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: The birthplace of modern Chinese astronomy, offering panoramic views of Nanjing and serving as a science education base.
China's first national institution of higher learning, a core scenic area of the Qinhuai River scenic belt, with well-preserved ancient architectural complexes.
A famous ancient temple in Nanjing, now a national revolutionary martyrs' cemetery, with a serene environment and the Linggu Pagoda as its landmark.
The mausoleum of Dr. Sun Yat-sen, a memorial site for the founding father of the Republic of China, and a core attraction of the Zhongshan Scenic Area.
The residence of Chiang Kai-shek and Soong Mei-ling, featuring French architectural style and known as the 'No. 1 Villa in the Far East.'
The largest imperial garden lake in China, praised as the 'Pearl of Jinling,' showcasing diverse scenery across all seasons.
A preserved historic district of Ming and Qing Dynasty architecture, blending traditional residences with modern commerce, offering an experience of old Nanjing's charm.
A sacred site commemorating the Chinese New Democratic Revolution, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
The tomb of Zhu Yuanzhang, the founding emperor of the Ming Dynasty, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a model of imperial tombs from the Ming and Qing Dynasties in China.
The ruins of an ancient temple from the Southern Dynasties, now a cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the Linggu Pagoda as its iconic structure.
A double-helix pedestrian bridge over the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark offering views of the river and the city skyline.
A famous snack street in Nanjing, gathering authentic local delicacies and recommended by locals as a must-visit food destination.
A Ming Dynasty pavilion-style building, a commanding point along the Yangtze River with expansive views.
Nanjing's most bustling commercial center, offering comprehensive shopping, dining, and entertainment options.
